chief_complaint: A term infant presenting with progressive developmental delay, feeding difficulties, recurrent seizures, and failure to thrive from early neonatal age.
history: The infant initially exhibited symptoms including vomiting, lethargy, and seizures, necessitating multiple hospitalisations. Progressive neurological deterioration, hepatomegaly, bilateral nephromegaly, hypothyroidism, gastro-oesophageal reflux disease, and auditory neuropathy were noted. MRI showed cerebral atrophy and dilated ventricles. Extensive investigations ruled out infections, metabolic acidosis, and structural brain malformations.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings: hepatomegaly, bilateral nephromegaly, and signs of developmental delay.
labs: Whole exome sequencing identified a homozygous missense mutation in the mannosyl-oligosaccharide glucosidase (MOGS) gene (c.2090T>C; p.Leu697Ser). MRI showed cerebral atrophy and dilated ventricles. Other investigations: normal ECG, normal bloods, normal imaging (excluding MRI findings).
correct_diagnosis: Congenital disorder of glycosylation type IIb
differentials: Congenital disorder of glycosylation type IIb
correct_ranking: Congenital disorder of glycosylation type IIb
